text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Phil. And I'm Georgie.
zh: [0] 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。我是Phil。我是Georgie。

text: Encountering an animal in its natural habitat is a magical experience.
zh: [1] 在自然栖息地遇见一只动物是一种奇妙的体验。

text: Have you ever seen an animal in the wild, Georgie? Yes, I have actually. I was on a walk and we saw a baby badger.
zh: [2] Georgie,你在野外见过动物吗?其实见过。我当时在散步,我们看到了一只小獾。

text: It was on its own and it was really cute. What about you, Phil?
zh: [3] 它当时独自一个,特别可爱。你呢,Phil?

text: The most impressive one I've ever seen is actually a whale.
zh: [4] 我见过的最令人印象深刻的动物其实是鲸鱼。

text: I was driving by the coast in Patagonia and you could see them in the sea.
zh: [5] 当时我沿着巴塔哥尼亚的海岸开车,能看到它们在海里。

text: Well, that sounds amazing. And funny you say that because whale watching has become popular in many coastal parts of the world.
zh: [6] 听起来太棒了。说到这个真巧,因为观鲸活动如今在世界许多沿海地区都变得很流行。

text: But watching whales is very different from what humans used to do –
zh: [7] 但观鲸和人类过去的做法非常不同——

text: hunt and kill them for their oil and meat, an activity known as whaling.
zh: [8] 那就是猎杀鲸鱼以获取鲸油和鲸肉,这种活动被称为捕鲸。

text: Last year marked the 40th anniversary of the International Whaling Commission's global moratorium.
zh: [9] 去年是国际捕鲸委员会全球禁令实施40周年。

text: That's a temporary ban on the commercial killing of whales.
zh: [10] 那是对商业捕鲸活动的临时禁令。

text: Although Japan, Iceland and Norway continue to hunt whales,
zh: [11] 尽管日本、冰岛和挪威仍在继续捕鲸,

text: there's no doubt the ban has saved many whale species from extinction.
zh: [12] 但毫无疑问,这项禁令挽救了许多鲸鱼物种,使它们免于灭绝。

text: In this program we'll discuss our past and present relationship
zh: [13] 在这期节目中,我们将讨论人类与

text: with these giants of the ocean and, as usual, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases too.
zh: [14] 这些海洋巨兽过去和现在的关系,并且像往常一样学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: Plus, you'll find all the vocabulary for this episode, along with a quiz and worksheet
zh: [15] 此外,你还能在我们的网站上找到本期节目的所有词汇,

text: on our website, bbclearningenglish .com. But first, I have a question for you,
zh: [16] 以及一份测验和练习题,网址是bbclearningenglish.com。不过首先,我有个问题要问你,

text: Georgie. You probably know that the world's largest whale is the blue whale, growing up to 30 metres long.
zh: [17] Georgie。你可能知道世界上最大的鲸鱼是蓝鲸,体长可达30米。

text: But which whale species is the smallest?
zh: [18] 但哪种鲸鱼是体型最小的呢?

text: list? Is it A. The beluga whale, B. The vaquita whale or C. The pygmy right whale?
zh: [19] A. 白鲸,B. 加湾鼠海豚,还是C. 小露脊鲸?

text: Well pygmy makes me think small so I'm going to go with C. The pygmy right whale. Okay we'll find out later in the program.
zh: [20] 嗯,pygmy(小型)让我联想到“小”,所以我选C,小露脊鲸。好,我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Humans have hunted whales for at least a thousand years, starting with small groups of hunters in slow moving wooden boats.
zh: [21] 人类捕鲸至少已有一千年历史,最早是由乘坐行驶缓慢的木船的小规模猎人开始的。

text: Catching whales like this was often unsuccessful,
zh: [22] 这样捕鲸常常并不成功,

text: especially big whales like blue and humpback whales, which can swim up to 30 kilometers per hour.
zh: [23] 尤其是像蓝鲸和座头鲸这样的大型鲸鱼,它们的游速可达每小时30公里。

text: But all that changed in the late 1800s as Jane Pearce of the South Georgia Heritage
zh: [24] 但到19世纪末,情况发生了变化,南乔治亚遗产信托基金会的Jane Pearce

text: Trust explained to BBC Radio 4 programme Rare Earth.
zh: [25] 向BBC第四台节目《稀土》做了这样的解释。

text: Something happened in Norway in the late 1800s.
zh: [26] 19世纪末,挪威发生了一件事。

text: A chap called Sven Boyne, who was a whaler, he invented what we now know as the the exploding harpoon
zh: [27] 一个叫Sven Foyn的人,他是个捕鲸者,发明了我们现在所知的爆炸鱼叉,

text: and that was a turning point for the whale. The whale couldn't stand a chance.
zh: [28] 那是鲸鱼命运的转折点。鲸鱼根本毫无胜算。

text: In 1870, a chap, that's an informal word for a man,
zh: [29] 1870年,一个chap(这是对男人的非正式说法),

text: called Sven Voin, invented the exploding harpoon, a weapon for killing whales.
zh: [30] 名叫Sven Foyn,发明了爆炸鱼叉,一种用于捕杀鲸鱼的武器。

text: This was a turning point. The phrase turning point means a time when a situation starts to change,
zh: [31] 这是一个转折点。“turning point”这个短语指的是情况开始发生变化的时刻,

text: especially in a positive way, although sadly not in this case.
zh: [32] 尤其是往好的方向发展,不过很遗憾这次并非如此。

text: After this, the whales didn't stand a chance, meaning they had no chance of successfully avoiding the hunters.
zh: [33] 从此以后,鲸鱼们毫无胜算,意思是它们完全没有机会成功躲避猎人。

text: Foyne's exploding harpoon, added to the invention of faster steam -powered
zh: [34] Foyn发明的爆炸鱼叉,加上蒸汽动力船的发明,

text: boats, increased whaling massively throughout the 20th century,
zh: [35] 使整个20世纪的捕鲸活动大幅增加,

text: to the point where over three million whales had been killed, and many species faced extinction.
zh: [36] 以至于超过三百万头鲸鱼被捕杀,许多物种濒临灭绝。

text: By the 1970s, there were only a few great whales left,
zh: [37] 到了20世纪70年代,大型鲸鱼所剩无几,

text: And environmental groups like Greenpeace started the Save the Whales campaign
zh: [38] 绿色和平等环保组织发起了“拯救鲸鱼”运动,

text: to make people aware of devastation in whale populations and the cruelty of whaling.
zh: [39] 让人们意识到鲸鱼数量遭到的毁灭性打击以及捕鲸行为的残忍。

text: It became popular to listen to the Song of the Whale, recordings of whales communicating at sea, which sounds something like this.
zh: [40] 聆听“鲸鱼之歌”——鲸鱼在海中交流的录音——一度非常流行,听起来大概是这样的。

text: Here's marine biologist Joe Roman
zh: [41] 下面是海洋生物学家Joe Roman

text: discussing the Save the Whales campaign with BBC Radio 4's Rare Earth. People started thinking about whales in different ways.
zh: [42] 与BBC第四台《稀土》节目讨论“拯救鲸鱼”运动的内容。人们开始以不同的方式看待鲸鱼。

text: And then we have to give a shout out to activist groups like Greenpeace, who put their rib boats,
zh: [43] 然后我们不得不为绿色和平这样的活动组织点个赞,他们把自己的橡皮艇,

text: they put their zodiacs in between Soviet hunting vessels and sperm whales, and they were very good at media.
zh: [44] 也就是充气艇,开到苏联捕鲸船和抹香鲸之间,而且他们非常擅长媒体宣传。

text: Joe gives a shout out to Greenpeace activists for protecting whales.
zh: [45] Joe为保护鲸鱼的绿色和平活动人士点赞。

text: To give a shout out to someone means to praise them in public.
zh: [46] “give a shout out to someone”意思是公开赞扬某人。

text: Videos and photos taken by Greenpeace changed public opinion about
zh: [47] 绿色和平拍摄的视频和照片改变了公众对

text: whaling. Jo says Greenpeace were good at media,
zh: [48] 捕鲸的看法。Joe说绿色和平“擅长媒体”,

text: meaning they skilfully used media outlets like TV and newspapers to convey their message to the public.
zh: [49] 意思是他们巧妙地利用电视、报纸等媒体渠道向公众传达他们的信息。

text: Many whale species are still endangered, and all whales still face danger from ocean acidification and plastic pollution.
zh: [50] 许多鲸鱼物种仍然濒临灭绝,而且所有鲸鱼仍然面临海洋酸化和塑料污染的威胁。

text: But the moratorium – that means the temporary suspension on hunting them – has lasted 40 years.
zh: [51] 但这项暂停令——也就是暂时禁止捕鲸——已经持续了40年,

text: and hopefully will help protect whales for decades more. OK, Phil, isn't it time you revealed the answer to your question?
zh: [52] 希望它还能在未来几十年里继续保护鲸鱼。好了,Phil,是不是该公布你那个问题的答案了?

text: OK, I asked you which was the smallest species of whale.
zh: [53] 好,我之前问你,哪种鲸鱼体型最小。

text: And I said the pygmy right whale, didn't I? You did.
zh: [54] 我说的是小露脊鲸,对吧?没错。

text: Unfortunately, the answer is actually the vaquita whale, which is about 1 .5 metres long.
zh: [55] 可惜答案其实是加湾鼠海豚,体长约1.5米。

text: Oh, so cute! OK, let's recap the rest of the vocabulary we've learnt.
zh: [56] 哦,好可爱!好了,我们来回顾一下今天学到的其余词汇。

text: A chap is an informal word for a man. A turning point is when a situation starts to change, especially for the better.
zh: [57] “chap”是对男人的非正式说法。“turning point”指情况开始发生变化的时刻,尤其是朝好的方向。

text: If someone doesn't stand a chance, they have no chance of success.
zh: [58] 如果某人“doesn't stand a chance”,意思是他们毫无成功的可能。

text: To give a shout out to someone means to publicly praise them.
zh: [59] “give a shout out to someone”意思是公开赞扬某人。

text: And finally, if you're good at media, you are skilful at using media outlets to convey a message to the public.
zh: [60] 最后,如果你“good at media”,意思是你擅长利用媒体渠道向公众传达信息。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember, you can find worksheets, quizzes and loads more resources
zh: [61] 我们的六分钟又到了尾声,不过别忘了,你可以在我们的网站

text: to improve your English on our website, site bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [62] bbclearningenglish.com上找到练习题、测验以及更多提升英语水平的资源。

text: See you there soon, but for now, it's goodbye. Bye!
zh: [63] 我们网站见,不过现在,再见啦。拜拜!
